title: Present simple vs present continuous
explanation:
Present simple vs present continuous (A2)

Present simple — routines, facts, permanent situations.

Present continuous — now, temporary situations, trends.

Signal words: every day, usually, always vs now, at the moment, today, this week.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.text: I [*usually walk|am usually walking|walk usually] to work.
She [*is studying|studies|study] for her exams this month.
They [*are staying|stay|stays] with friends this week.
He [*doesn't usually eat|isn't usually eating|not usually eats] breakfast.
Listen! Someone [*is knocking|knocks|knock] at the door.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Where's Anna? She ____ a shower. | *is having | has | have
My parents ____ in a small village. | *live | are living | lives
I ____ to learn Spanish this year. | *am trying | try | tries
The sun ____ in the east. | *rises | is rising | rise

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.feedback: Fixed plan → present continuous for future.

questions.text: He [*plays|is playing|play] the guitar every evening.
